nodes.name: Receive Company Data
nodes.notes: Receives company data from the queue. Expected body: { run_id, company_name, company_domain }

nodes.name: Has Domain?
nodes.notes: Routes to cache check if company_domain exists, else error

nodes.name: Check Scrape Cache

parameters.query: SELECT $1::text AS cache_key, response_data, TRUE AS cache_hit FROM scrape_cache WHERE cache_key = $1 AND source_type = 'company_profile' AND expires_at > NOW() UNION ALL SELECT $1::text AS cache_key, NULL::jsonb AS response_data, FALSE AS cache_hit WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM scrape_cache WHERE cache_key = $1 AND source_type = 'company_profile' AND expires_at > NOW()) LIMIT 1

nodes.notes: Always returns 1 row. cache_hit=true if fresh cache exists, else cache_hit=false

nodes.name: Cache Fresh?
nodes.notes: true = use cached data, false = start Firecrawl crawl

nodes.name: Start Firecrawl Crawl
nodes.notes: Initiates async Firecrawl crawl job. Returns { id, url } for polling

nodes.name: Poll Crawl Status
nodes.notes: Polls GET /v1/crawl/{id} every 5s for up to 60 iterations. Returns { crawlSuccess, crawlData } or { crawlSuccess: false, error }

nodes.name: Crawl Success?
nodes.notes: true = extract structured data, false = fallback to search

nodes.name: Firecrawl - Extract Data

parameters.name: extract
parameters.value: ={{ { prompt: 'Extract the company name, description, headquarters location, industry sector, employee count range, list of products and services, key executives, mission statement, and technology stack from this company website' } }}

nodes.notes: Scrapes the company domain with structured data extraction prompt

nodes.name: Firecrawl - Search Fallback

parameters.name: query
parameters.value: ={{ $node['Receive Company Data'].json.company_name + ' ' + $node['Receive Company Data'].json.company_domain + ' company profile information about products services leadership' }}

nodes.notes: Fallback when crawl fails. Uses Firecrawl search to find company info

nodes.name: Insert into Scrape Cache

parameters.query: INSERT INTO scrape_cache (cache_key, source_type, company_domain, response_data, expires_at, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4::jsonb, NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days', NOW()) ON CONFLICT (cache_key, source_type) DO UPDATE SET response_data = $4::jsonb, expires_at = NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days', updated_at = NOW() RETURNING *

nodes.notes: Upserts Firecrawl results into scrape_cache with 7-day TTL

nodes.name: Update Queue - Completed

parameters.query: UPDATE scenario_queue SET status = $1, output_data = $2::jsonb, updated_at = NOW() WHERE run_id = $3

nodes.notes: Marks queue item completed with the output data

nodes.name: Update Queue - Cached

parameters.query: UPDATE scenario_queue SET status = $1, output_data = $2::jsonb, updated_at = NOW() WHERE run_id = $3

nodes.notes: Marks queue item completed using cached data

nodes.name: Update Queue - Error

parameters.query: UPDATE scenario_queue SET status = $1, output_data = $2::jsonb, updated_at = NOW() WHERE run_id = $3
parameters.queryParams: ={{ ['error', JSON.stringify({ error: 'No company domain provided', company_name: $json.company_name, company_domain: $json.company_domain }), $json.run_id] }}

nodes.notes: Marks queue item as error when no domain is provided
